title: Samsung strongly hints at Galaxy Z Fold8, Z Fold8 Ultra, and Z Flip8 unveiling date
description: A couple of rumors so far have suggested that Samsung will hold its next big Unpacked event on July 22 in London, and now the company itself is strongly hinting at the fact that it is the correct date. Over in Malaysia, Samsung is offering its loyal fans an exclusive MYR 700 ($171) discount "on the purchase of their next Galaxy Foldable". This will be available in the Samsung Members app from July 22 until October 4. The fact that the code is going to start appearing on July 22 is almost an official confirmation that it is in fact the date of the next Unpacked event. So hopefully...

title: Commodore Callback 8020 goes on pre-order for a lower price
description: Commodore announced its $499 Callback 8020 flip phone a couple of weeks ago, and apparently people didn't like the price. So now the device has gone up for pre-order as promised, but it starts at $399. That's some magic trick - making the product 20% cheaper before even launching it. Part of this is explained by the fact that the $399 price includes "eco-validated" RAM and storage, which means they're "remanufactured", aka reused, aka recycled, whatever you want to call it. If you want your RAM and storage to be new, you need to pay an extra $50 and then you're up to $449. Anyway,...

title: Samsung ultimately refuses BOE panels for the Galaxy S27, new report says
description: Last month we heard that Samsung was in talks with China’s BOE for the supply of OLED panels for the upcoming Galaxy S27, and BOE's displays were allegedly $5 cheaper than what Samsung Display could supply. So it seemed pretty inevitable that BOE would get the deal and the vanilla S27 would ship with the Chinese panels. Well, not so fast. A plot twist has emerged today. Apparently Samsung has decided not to go with the BOE displays after all, and so Samsung Display was chosen to supply the OLED panels for the Galaxy S27. While a clear reason for this decision is not known, there's...

title: Samsung confirms the Galaxy Ring 2
description: Samsung launched the Galaxy Ring in 2024, and so you may have been wondering where its successor is. Back in May we heard that the Galaxy Ring 2 would arrive in early 2027 with improved battery life, comfort, and sensor accuracy. Now a new report says Samsung itself has confirmed that the Galaxy Ring 2 is in development, through the voice of Dr. Hon Pak, the company's head of the Digital Health team. The original Samsung Galaxy Ring He strongly hinted at upcoming iPhone pairing support, while confirming that the next Galaxy Ring will have longer battery life and upgraded health...

title: Motorola Edge 70 Max gets certified with Qi2 25W wireless charging
description: Motorola is expected to expand its Edge 70 lineup soon with the Edge 70 Max, and while the brand hasn't revealed when it will unveil the smartphone, the Edge 70 Max has been certified by the Wireless Power Consortium (WPC), taking it a step closer to launch. The WPC listing also reveals that the Motorola Edge 70 Max, bearing model code XT2611, will support Qi2 25W magnetic wireless charging. It has Qi ID 26944, Qi version 2.2.1, and MPP25 power profile. Motorola Edge 70 Max gets Qi2 25W certified by WPC MPP25 means the Edge 70 Max has built-in magnets for wireless charging up to...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8, Z Fold8 Ultra, and Z Flip8 cases leaks online
description: As the rumored launch of Samsung's next-generation foldables draws closer, leaks have started to ramp up. The latest one showcases a set of cases for the Galaxy Z Fold8, Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, and Galaxy Z Flip8. The leak showcases a wide range of cases for Samsung's upcoming foldables, suggesting these accessories could be available alongside the phones at launch. For the flagship Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Samsung is likely to offer several options such as clear cases, silicone cases and aramid fiber cases. The leak also shows aramid fiber cases with integrated...

title: RedMagic Astra 2 debuts as Gaming Tablet 5 Pro in China
description: The RedMagic Gaming Tablet 5 Pro has a ridiculously long name, but it might just be the best-specced Android tablet in 2026. Coming in as a successor to last year’s RedMagic Astra, the new edition brings the latest Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset, which is configurable with up to 16GB RAM and 1TB UFS 4.1 Pro storage. Like its predecessor, the new device is launching in China first and is set to make its way internationally as the RedMagic Astra 2. The new device features the same-sized 9.06-inch OLED with 2.4K resolution (1,504 x 2,400px) and a snappier 185Hz refresh rate,...

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 key features revealed
description: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 and Z Flip8 series are just around the corner, and the intensity of leaks grows. The latest one comes from a reputable source, revealing some Galaxy Z Fold8 key specs. If you haven't been following the story, keep in mind that the new Galaxy Z Fold8 isn't a direct successor to the Galaxy Z Fold7 from last year. It's actually the previously rumored "Galaxy Fold Wide", which will take the place of the vanilla, while the current Z Fold7's true successor will be the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra. Reportedly, Samsung decided to change the naming scheme back in...

title: Redmi K90 Ultra is here with the Snapdragon 8 Elite and a massive built-in active cooling fan
description: Today in China, Redmi made the K90 Ultra official, ending many months of leaks, rumors, and speculation regarding the device. The K90 Ultra comes with the older Snapdragon 8 Elite chipset at the helm, but it pairs it with a built-in active cooling fan which is claimed to lower the temperature by up to 10°C, ensuring that long gaming sessions won't see any throttling. There's also a standalone D2 interpolation chip for gaming. The phone boasts a 6.83-inch 1280x2772 flat AMOLED panel with 165Hz refresh rate, 3,500-nit peak brightness, and full array DC dimming, a 50MP main camera with OIS...

title: iPhone 18 Pro emerges in leaked drop test
description: In a very un-Apple manner, the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro emerged in the flesh well ahead of its announcement. The yet-to-be-released flagship appeared in a drop test showcasing its design which, as expected, is nearly identical to the iPhone 17 Pro. There are two videos circulating the web and both showcase controlled environment drops of the iPhone 18 Pro. The videos were shared by @evleaks on X, though it appears that account is not related to Evan Blass, and it has since been suspended. iPhone 18 Pro leaked drop test As for how these videos found their way...

title: Samsung Galaxy A27 launched in India
description: The Samsung Galaxy A27, unveiled last week, made its India debut today. The smartphone comes in Light Green, Light Pink, and Black colors in India, with three memory configurations - 6GB/128GB, 8GB/128GB, and 8GB/256GB, priced at INR31,999, INR34,999, and INR40,499, respectively. [#InlinePriceWidget,14606,1#] The Galaxy A27 will go on sale in India starting July 3 through Samsung's official website, exclusive outlets, partner stores, and other online platforms. Samsung said all models are eligible for INR3,000 cashback on bank/UPI payments, but didn't reveal for how long. Anyway,...

title: New leak details the cameras on Samsung's upcoming Galaxy Z foldables
description: Samsung is expected to unveil its next generation of Galaxy Z foldables in July. The lineup is rumored to include the Galaxy Z Fold8 wide, Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, and Galaxy Z Flip8. Ahead of the expected launch, a new leak has revealed the key camera specifications of all three devices. According to the leak, the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra will retain the Galaxy Z Fold7's triple rear camera setup, but replace the 12MP ultrawide camera with a new 50MP sensor. Dummy units of the Galaxy Z Fold8 wide (left) and Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra (right) The foldable is otherwise expected to keep the same...

title: OnePlus N6 arrives with 8,000mAh battery and Dimensity 6360 Max
description: There’s a brand-new OnePlus smartphone series and India is the first market to get it. The standout feature on the OnePlus N6 is its massive 8,000mAh battery. It supports 45W SuperVOOC wired charging as well as 5W reverse wired charging, so you can use the N6 as a powerbank. OnePlus also incorporated bypass charging, so the N6 can draw power directly to its motherboard without it ever reaching the battery. This helps keep the device cool during intensive tasks like gaming. OnePlus proudly claims the N6’s battery is engineered to survive over 1,600 charge cycles and retain over 80% of...

title: Qualcomm announces Snapdragon Summit 2026 dates
description: Qualcomm has announced it will host its annual Snapdragon Summit from September 22-24 in Maui, Hawaii. While Qualcomm hasn't revealed what it will announce at this Snapdragon Summit, it's likely the US-based chipmaker will introduce a successor to last year's flagship Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 SoC, rumored to be called the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6 (SM8950). However, that might not be the only mobile platform Qualcomm will announce at the Summit, as it's rumored to introduce the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6 Pro (SM8975) as well. A recent leak revealed that Qualcomm is also working on...

title: iQOO tipped to be working on a compact gaming tablet
description: iQOO could be gearing up to launch a compact gaming tablet in the coming months, according to a new leak. According to tipster Digital Chat Station, iQOO's upcoming compact gaming tablet will be powered by Qualcomm's next-generation flagship Snapdragon chipset, although the exact SoC wasn't specified. iQOO Pad 6 Pro The tipster also claims the tablet will debut in the second half of the year. It is expected to launch alongside one of iQOO's flagship smartphones, likely the rumored iQOO 16, which is tipped to arrive in September. The gaming tablet is likely to feature a display...

title: Nothing Phone (4b) live images show all three colors, confirm key specs
description: Just a few hours ago, Nothing confirmed that its upcoming Phone (4b) will be powered by a Snapdragon chipset, without going so far as to reveal which one. Meanwhile, an X user seems to have gotten his hands on all three colors of the Nothing Phone (4b) and decided to show them off to the world in live image form. Here they are in all their glory: Nothing Phone (4b) live images The same source has also shared images showing the About Phone section, which confirms a bunch of things about the device. It is running Nothing OS 4.1 based on Android 16. Nothing Phone (4b)...

title: WhatsApp username reservations go live this week
description: WhatsApp is finally, after years of testing and anticipation, introducing usernames. You will be able to reserve yours starting "this week". The usernames themselves will only roll out "later this year" though, in a gradual manner. WhatsApp will notify you when they're available in your country. Obviously, with over three billion users, a lot of people will want the same username, so it's going to be a wild ride. WhatsApp says it's adding usernames because "a phone number is personal and it's tied to so many parts of your life", and "sometimes you just want to chat without handing over...

title: Comcast is spinning off NBCUniversal and Sky
description: Comcast is spinning off NBCUniversal and Sky into a new company. The split will leave Comcast with the cable, wireless, and business services, while the new company will handle the Universal theme parks, film and TV studios, NBC, Peacock, and Sky. This move practically undoes the 2011 acquisition of NBCUniversal by Comcast. So after 15 years of consolidation, it's now time for some de-consolidation. Interestingly, Comcast CEO Brian Roberts will remain "actively involved" in leading both companies after the split. Roberts, whose father Ralph founded the company in 1963, controls...

title: BT and Verizon agree to combine their international enterprise operations
description: Verizon and BT Group have announced plans to combine their international enterprise fixed-line operations into a new joint venture. The move is expected to close in 2027, subject to regulatory approval. BT and Verizon say the new company will be "designed for a cloud-first world in the age of AI", whatever that means. It will be a 50-50 joint venture, and will serve over 3,000 multinational organizations in more than 180 countries. Verizon will pay BT an equalization amount of $625 million to guarantee even voting rights. Combining BT and Verizon’s international enterprise...

title: Motorola Razr Fold review
